trends.title: Raw Texture & Organic Forms
trends.description: Unpolished, organic textures are dominating high-end jewelry content. Think hammered silver, rough-cut stones, and imperfect beauty. Brands like Alighieri and Completedworks are leading this aesthetic.
trends.suggestion: Photograph pieces on natural stone, linen, or unfinished wood. Let the silver texture be the hero.

trends.title: Dark & Moody Product Shots
trends.description: The 'Cartier Noir' approach is trending hard — deep blacks, dramatic side-lighting, jewelry floating in darkness. Creates immediate luxury perception.
trends.suggestion: Use the Cartier Noir creative direction for hero pieces. Works especially well for statement rings and bold pendants.

trends.title: Heritage Storytelling Carousels
trends.description: Multi-slide posts that tell the origin story — from raw material to finished piece. Consumers want to know the craft behind luxury goods. Moroccan heritage is a unique differentiator.
trends.suggestion: Create a carousel: Slide 1: finished piece, Slide 2: workshop detail, Slide 3: artisan hands, Slide 4: heritage pattern inspiration.

trends.title: Clean White Catalog Aesthetics
trends.description: Alternating dark moody shots with clean white product shots creates visual rhythm in the feed. Tiffany, Mejuri, and Ana Luisa all use this contrast strategy.
trends.suggestion: For every 2 dark posts, do 1 clean white. Use Tiffany Ice or Studio Clean direction.

trends.title: Extreme Close-Up Macro Details
trends.description: Zoomed-in shots of engravings, clasps, and surface textures perform 40% better engagement than full-product shots. The detail IS the content.
trends.suggestion: Take extreme close-ups of your best detailing work. Sterling silver grain and Moroccan patterns are perfect for this.

trends.title: Styled Life Context
trends.description: Jewelry shown in lifestyle context (on hands pouring coffee, against fabric, with flowers) outperforms isolated product shots for engagement. But isolated shots win for conversion.
trends.suggestion: Mix 60% styled context + 40% isolated product in your content calendar.

trends.title: Process Reveal Reels
trends.description: Short-form video showing the making process is the #1 performing content type for jewelry brands on Instagram in 2026.
trends.suggestion: Even 10-second clips of polishing, setting, or packaging drive massive engagement. Consider filming in your workshop.

trends.title: Color-Blocked Feed Planning
trends.description: Top brands plan their feed visually — alternating color themes in rows of 3. Dark-Light-Dark or Warm-Cool-Warm creates a cohesive, curated gallery effect.
trends.suggestion: Plan content in sets of 3. Use the creative directions to alternate: Cartier Noir → Tiffany Ice → Heritage Gold.

contentCalendarTip: Post 4-5 times per week. Best times for jewelry: Tuesday 10am, Thursday 2pm, Saturday 11am (Morocco time / CET).
